name: Gandhara Sculpture

visual_attributes.morphology: Sculptural representations of Buddhist figures with Greco-Roman stylistic influences
distinctive_features: First representations of Gautama Buddha in human form
Greco-Roman elements such as drapery and anatomical precision
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: Varies from small reliefs to large statues, typically life-sized or larger
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: Figures often depicted in frontal poses with detailed backgrounds

domain_specific_functional.Symbolism: Represents the spread of Buddhism and cultural exchange
domain_specific_functional.Historical_significance: Marks the end of aniconism in Buddhist art

relationships.description: Gandhara Sculpture incorporates elements of Hellenistic art such as realism and anatomical precision.
Gandhara Sculpture often depicts the Buddha in human form, a significant development in Buddhist art.

contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Represents the fusion of Greek and Buddhist artistic traditions

contrastive_constraints: NOT Purely Indian or Greek Art
